headline: Meta +8% on AI compute cloud push
detail: Meta's surge on selling excess AI compute capacity signals strong enterprise AI demand and validates AI infrastructure spending. This is positive sentiment for the broader tech complex and could lift names like CRWD and AMD in sympathy. However it's a software/cloud catalyst, not a direct semiconductor revenue driver, so SMH and SOXL may not move as much.
rec: Nothing to buy directly off this - it's not a chip catalyst. Watch if CRWD (already up 7.56%, scanner shows 765 entry, stop 755.85) extends its move; if it does, your existing position benefits, but don't chase a new add into daily premium.

headline: U.S. auto industry faces USMCA uncertainty
detail: Trade deal uncertainty adds another layer of macro risk to supply chains broadly. While your book is mostly software/semis, broader trade anxiety can weigh on risk appetite and hit leveraged positions hardest. Not a direct hit to your names but adds background noise for margin traders.
rec: No direct play for your holdings - but it's a reason to stay disciplined on your leveraged ETF stops (SOXL, SPAL) rather than holding through macro volatility hoping for a bounce.

headline: Palantir's Karp bashes OpenAI and Anthropic token model
detail: Karp's comments highlight competitive tension in enterprise AI but don't directly affect your holdings. It keeps AI narrative debate alive, which can create intraday volatility in AI-adjacent names. No specific impact on CRWD, SMH, or your chip names.
rec: Nothing actionable for your book - monitor if AI narrative sentiment shifts broader tech mood, but no trade warranted from this headline alone.
